entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Average Population
entry.content:
Query the average population for all cities in CITY, rounded down to the nearest integer.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

Solution

SELECT FLOOR(AVG(POPULATION)) FROM CITY;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Average Population of Each Continent
entry.content:
Given the CITY and COUNTRY tables, query the names of all the continents (COUNTRY.Continent) and their respective average city populations (CITY.Population) rounded down to the nearest integer.

Note: CITY.CountryCode and COUNTRY.Code are matching key columns.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

The COUNTRY table is described as follows:

Field Type
CODE VARCHAR2(3)
NAME VARCHAR2(44)
CONTINENT VARCHAR2(13)
REGION VARCHAR2(25)
SURFACEAREA NUMBER
INDEPYEAR VARCHAR2(5)
POPULATION NUMBER
LIFEEXPECTANCY VARCHAR2(4)
GNP NUMBER
GNPOLD VARCHAR2(9)
LOCALNAME VARCHAR2(44)
GOVERMENTFORM VARCHAR2(44)
HEADOFSTATE VARCHAR2(32)
CAPITAL VARCHAR2(4)
CODE2 VARCHAR2(2)

Solution

SELECT COUNTRY.CONTINENT, FLOOR(AVG(CITY.POPULATION))
FROM CITY
JOIN COUNTRY ON CITY.COUNTRYCODE = COUNTRY.CODE
GROUP BY COUNTRY.CONTINENT;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Asian Population
entry.content:
Given the CITY and COUNTRY tables, query the sum of the populations of all cities where the CONTINENT is 'Asia'.

Note: CITY.CountryCode and COUNTRY.Code are matching key columns.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

The COUNTRY table is described as follows:

Field Type
CODE VARCHAR2(3)
NAME VARCHAR2(44)
CONTINENT VARCHAR2(13)
REGION VARCHAR2(25)
SURFACEAREA NUMBER
INDEPYEAR VARCHAR2(5)
POPULATION NUMBER
LIFEEXPECTANCY VARCHAR2(4)
GNP NUMBER
GNPOLD VARCHAR2(9)
LOCALNAME VARCHAR2(44)
GOVERMENTFORM VARCHAR2(44)
HEADOFSTATE VARCHAR2(32)
CAPITAL VARCHAR2(4)
CODE2 VARCHAR2(2)

Solution

SELECT SUM(CITY.POPULATION)
FROM CITY
JOIN COUNTRY ON CITY.COUNTRYCODE = COUNTRY.CODE
WHERE COUNTRY.CONTINENT = 'ASIA';

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Draw The Triangle 1
entry.content:
Problem

P(R) represents a pattern drawn by Julia in R rows. The following pattern represents P(5):

* * * * * 
* * * * 
* * * 
* * 
*

Write a query to print the pattern P(20).

Solution

Platform: MySQL

SET @TEMP:=21; 
SELECT REPEAT('* ', @TEMP:= @TEMP - 1) 
FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES;

entry.title: SQL LeetCode 183 Customers Who Never Order
entry.content:
Problem

Suppose that a website contains two tables, the Customers table and the Orders table. Write a SQL query to find all customers who never order anything.

Table: Customers.

+----+-------+
| Id | Name  |
+----+-------+
| 1  | Joe   |
| 2  | Henry |
| 3  | Sam   |
| 4  | Max   |
+----+-------+

Table: Orders.

+----+------------+
| Id | CustomerId |
+----+------------+
| 1  | 3          |
| 2  | 1          |
+----+------------+

Using the above tables as example, return the following:

+-----------+
| Customers |
+-----------+
| Henry     |
| Max       |
+-----------+

Solution

SELECT Customers.Name AS Customers
FROM Customers LEFT JOIN Orders
ON Orders.CustomerId = Customers.Id
WHERE Orders.CustomerId IS NULL;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Draw The Triangle 2
entry.content:
Problem

P(R) represents a pattern drawn by Julia in R rows. The following pattern represents P(5):

* 
* * 
* * * 
* * * * 
* * * * *

Write a query to print the pattern P(20).

Solution

Platform: MySQL

SET @TEMP:=0; 
SELECT REPEAT('* ', @TEMP:= @TEMP + 1) 
FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES
WHERE @TEMP < 20;

entry.title: SQL LeetCode 182 Duplicate Emails
entry.content:
Problem

Write a SQL query to find all duplicate emails in a table named Person.

+----+---------+
| Id | Email   |
+----+---------+
| 1  | a@b.com |
| 2  | c@d.com |
| 3  | a@b.com |
+----+---------+

For example, your query should return the following for the above table:

+---------+
| Email   |
+---------+
| a@b.com |
+---------+

Note: All emails are in lowercase.

Solution

SELECT Email FROM Person 
GROUP BY Email
Having count(*) > 1;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Employee Names
entry.content:
Problem

Write a query that prints a list of employee names (i.e.: the name attribute) from the Employee table in alphabetical order.

Input Format

The Employee table containing employee data for a company is described as follows:

Column Type
employee_id Integer
name String
months Integer
salary Integer

where employee_id is an employee’s ID number, name is their name, months is the total number of months they’ve been working for the company, and salary is their monthly salary.

Sample Input

employee_id name months salary
12228 Rose 15 1968
33645 Angela 1 3443
45692 Frank 17 1608
56118 Patrick 7 1345
59725 Lisa 11 2330
74197 Kimberly 16 4372
78454 Bonnie 8 1771
83565 Michael 6 2017
98607 Todd 5 3396
99989 Joe 9 3573

Sample Output

Angela
Bonnie
Frank
Joe
Kimberly
Lisa
Michael
Patrick
Rose
Todd

Solution

SELECT NAME FROM EMPLOYEE
ORDER BY NAME;

entry.title: ISLR 第一章读书笔记
SQL Hackerrank African Cities
entry.content:
Given the CITY and COUNTRY tables, query the names of all cities where the CONTINENT is 'Africa'.

Note: CITY.CountryCode and COUNTRY.Code are matching key columns.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

The COUNTRY table is described as follows:

Field Type
CODE VARCHAR2(3)
NAME VARCHAR2(44)
CONTINENT VARCHAR2(13)
REGION VARCHAR2(25)
SURFACEAREA NUMBER
INDEPYEAR VARCHAR2(5)
POPULATION NUMBER
LIFEEXPECTANCY VARCHAR2(4)
GNP NUMBER
GNPOLD VARCHAR2(9)
LOCALNAME VARCHAR2(44)
GOVERMENTFORM VARCHAR2(44)
HEADOFSTATE VARCHAR2(32)
CAPITAL VARCHAR2(4)
CODE2 VARCHAR2(2)

Solution

SELECT CITY.NAME
FROM CITY
JOIN COUNTRY ON CITY.COUNTRYCODE = COUNTRY.CODE
WHERE COUNTRY.CONTINENT = 'AFRICA';

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Employee Salaries
entry.content:
Problem

Write a query that prints a list of employee names (i.e.: the name attribute) for employees in Employeehaving a salary greater than $2000 per month who have been employees for less than 10 months. Sort your result by ascending employee_id.

Input Format

The Employee table containing employee data for a company is described as follows:

Column Type
employee_id Integer
name String
months Integer
salary Integer

where employee_id is an employee’s ID number, name is their name, months is the total number of months they’ve been working for the company, and salary is their monthly salary.

Sample Input

employee_id name months salary
12228 Rose 15 1968
33645 Angela 1 3443
45692 Frank 17 1608
56118 Patrick 7 1345
59725 Lisa 11 2330
74197 Kimberly 16 4372
78454 Bonnie 8 1771
83565 Michael 6 2017
98607 Todd 5 3396
99989 Joe 9 3573

Sample Output

Angela
Michael
Todd
Joe

Explanation

  • Angela has been an employee for 1 month and earns $3443 per month.
  • Michael has been an employee for 6 months and earns $2017 per month.
  • Todd has been an employee for 5 months and earns $3396 per month.
  • Joe has been an employee for 9 months and earns $3573 per month.

We order our output by ascending employee_id.

Idea

  • Employeehaving a salary greater than $2000 per month who have been employees for less than 10 months. Use where clause WHERE SALARY > 2000 AND MONTHS < 10.
  • Sort your result by ascending employee_id, ORDER BY EMPLOYEE_ID.

Solution

SELECT NAME FROM EMPLOYEE
WHERE SALARY > 2000 AND MONTHS < 10
ORDER BY EMPLOYEE_ID;

entry.title: SQL LeetCode 181 Employees Earning More Than Their Managers
entry.content:
Problem

The Employee table holds all employees including their managers. Every employee has an Id, and there is also a column for the manager Id.

+----+-------+--------+-----------+
| Id | Name  | Salary | ManagerId |
+----+-------+--------+-----------+
| 1  | Joe   | 70000  | 3         |
| 2  | Henry | 80000  | 4         |
| 3  | Sam   | 60000  | NULL      |
| 4  | Max   | 90000  | NULL      |
+----+-------+--------+-----------+

Given the Employee table, write a SQL query that finds out employees who earn more than their managers. For the above table, Joe is the only employee who earns more than his manager.

+----------+
| Employee |
+----------+
| Joe      |
+----------+

Solution

SELECT A.Name AS Employee
FROM Employee A
LEFT JOIN Employee B
ON A.ManagerId = B.Id
WHERE A.Salary > B.Salary;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Population Density Difference
entry.content:
Query the difference between the maximum and minimum populations in CITY table.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)

FROM CITY;

entry.title: SQL LeetCode 175 Combine Two Tables
entry.content:
Problem

Table: Person

+-------------+---------+
| Column Name | Type    |
+-------------+---------+
| PersonId    | int     |
| FirstName   | varchar |
| LastName    | varchar |
+-------------+---------+
PersonId is the primary key column for this table.

Table: Address

+-------------+---------+
| Column Name | Type    |
+-------------+---------+
| AddressId   | int     |
| PersonId    | int     |
| City        | varchar |
| State       | varchar |
+-------------+---------+
AddressId is the primary key column for this table.

Write a SQL query for a report that provides the following information for each person in the Person table, regardless if there is an address for each of those people:

FirstName, LastName, City, State

Solution

SELECT FirstName, LastName, City, State
FROM Person
LEFT JOIN Address ON Person.PersonId = Address.PersonId;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Revising Aggregations - Averages
entry.content:
Query the average population of all cities in CITY where District is California.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

Solution

SELECT AVG(POPULATION) FROM CITY
WHERE DISTRICT = 'CALIFORNIA';

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Revising Aggregations - The Sum Function

NAME VARCHAR2(17)

DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)

WHERE DISTRICT = 'CALIFORNIA';

entry.title: SQL LeetCode 197 Rising Temperature
entry.content:
Problem

Given a Weather table, write a SQL query to find all dates’ Ids with higher temperature compared to its previous (yesterday’s) dates.

+---------+------------------+------------------+
| Id(INT) | RecordDate(DATE) | Temperature(INT) |
+---------+------------------+------------------+
|       1 |       2015-01-01 |               10 |
|       2 |       2015-01-02 |               25 |
|       3 |       2015-01-03 |               20 |
|       4 |       2015-01-04 |               30 |
+---------+------------------+------------------+

For example, return the following Ids for the above Weather table:

+----+
| Id |
+----+
|  2 |
|  4 |
+----+

Solution

SELECT A.Id
FROM Weather A
LEFT JOIN Weather B
ON TO_DAYS(A.RecordDate) - 1 = TO_DAYS(B.RecordDate) 
WHERE A.Temperature > B.Temperature;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Revising Aggregations - The Count Function
entry.content:
Query a count of the number of cities in CITY having a Population larger than 100,000.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

Solution

SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM CITY
WHERE POPULATION > 100000;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Type of Triangle
entry.content:
Problem

Write a query identifying the type of each record in the TRIANGLES table using its three side lengths. Output one of the following statements for each record in the table:

  • Equilateral: It’s a triangle with 3 sides of equal length.
  • Isosceles: It’s a triangle with 2 sides of equal length.
  • Scalene: It’s a triangle with 1 sides of differing lengths.
  • Not A Triangle: The given values of A, B, and C don’t form a triangle.

Input Format

The TRIANGLES table is described as follows:

Column Type
A Integer
B Integer
C Integer

Each row in the table denotes the lengths of each of a triangle’s three sides.

Sample Input

A B C
20 20 23
20 20 20
20 21 22
13 14 30

Sample Output

Isosceles
Equilateral
Scalene
Not A Triangle

Explanation

  • Values in the tuple $(20,20,23)$ form an Isosceles triangle, because $A=B$.
  • Values in the tuple $(20,20,20)$ form an Equilateral triangle, because $A=B=C$.
  • Values in the tuple $(20,21,22)$ form a Scalene triangle, because $A\ne B\ne C$ and $A\ne C$.
  • Values in the tuple $(13,14),30$ cannot form a triangle because the combined value of sides A and B is not larger than that of side C.

Idea

This is a math problem. If the tuple $(A,B,C)$ form a triagnle, we must have
$$(A+B-C)(A+C-B)(B+C-A)>0.$$To see this, suppose $A$ is the largest side, then $A+B-C>0$ and $A+C-B>0$. If we get a triagnle, then we must have $B+C-A>0$. Now we are ready for the answer.

  • Use CASE and WHEN (analogous to if and else if).
  • First, we consider the case if it does not form a triangle. Then we mush have (A+B-C)*(A+C-B)*(B+C-A) <= 0.
  • Second, we consider the case all sides are equal.
  • Then, only two sides are equal. Note that this is if and else if, this only happens if (A-B)*(B-C)*(C-A) = 0.
  • Finally, the rest are 'Scalene'.

Solution

SELECT
CASE
    WHEN (A+B-C)*(A+C-B)*(B+C-A) <= 0 THEN 'Not A Triangle'
    WHEN A = B AND A = C THEN 'Equilateral'
    WHEN (A-B)*(B-C)*(C-A) = 0 THEN 'Isosceles'
    ELSE 'Scalene'
END AS TYPE
FROM TRIANGLES;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank The Blunder
entry.content:
Problem

Samantha was tasked with calculating the average monthly salaries for all employees in the EMPLOYEES table, but did not realize her keyboard’s 0 key was broken until after completing the calculation. She wants your help finding the difference between her miscalculation (using salaries with any zeroes removed), and the actual average salary.

Write a query calculating the amount of error (i.e.: actual - miscalculated average monthly salaries), and round it up to the next integer.

Input Format

The Employee table containing employee data for a company is described as follows:

Column Type
ID Integer
Name String
salary Integer

Note: Salary is measured in dollars per month and its value is $<10^5$.

Sample Input

ID Name salary
1 Kristeen 1420
2 Ashley 2006
3 Julia 2210
4 Maria 3000

Sample Output

2061

Explanation

The table below shows the salaries without zeroes as they were entered by Samantha:

ID Name salary
1 Kristeen 142
2 Ashley 26
3 Julia 221
4 Maria 3

Samantha computes an average salary of 98.00. The actual average salary is 2159.00.

The resulting error between the two calculations is 2159.00 - 98.00 = 2061.00 which, when rounded to the next integer, is .

Solution

SELECT CEILING(
    AVG(SALARY) - 
    AVG(REPLACE(SALARY, '0', ''))
)
FROM EMPLOYEES;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Top Earners
entry.content:
Problem

We define an employee’s total earnings to be their monthly salary$\times$months worked, and the maximum total earnings to be the maximum total earnings for any employee in the Employee table. Write a query to find the maximum total earnings for all employees as well as the total number of employees who have maximum total earnings. Then print these values as space-separated integers.

Input Format

The Employee table containing employee data for a company is described as follows:

Column Type
employee_id Integer
name String
months Integer
salary Integer

where employee_id is an employee’s ID number, name is their name, months is the total number of months they’ve been working for the company, and salary is their monthly salary.

Sample Input

employee_id name months salary
12228 Rose 15 1968
33645 Angela 1 3443
45692 Frank 17 1608
56118 Patrick 7 1345
59725 Lisa 11 2330
74197 Kimberly 16 4372
78454 Bonnie 8 1771
83565 Michael 6 2017
98607 Todd 5 3396
99989 Joe 9 3573

Sample Output

69952 1

Explanation

The table and earnings data is depicted in the following diagram:

employee_id name months salary earnings
12228 Rose 15 1968 29520
33645 Angela 1 3443 3443
45692 Frank 17 1608 27336
56118 Patrick 7 1345 9415
59725 Lisa 11 2330 25630
74197 Kimberly 16 4372 69952
78454 Bonnie 8 1771 14168
83565 Michael 6 2017 12012
98607 Todd 5 3396 16980
99989 Joe 9 3573 32157

The maximum earnings value is 69952. The only employee with earnings =69952 is Kimberly, so we print the maximum earnings value (69952) and a count of the number of employees who have earned $69952 (which is 1) as two space-separated values.

Solution

SELECT SALARY * MONTHS, COUNT(*)
FROM EMPLOYEE
GROUP BY SALARY * MONTHS
ORDER BY SALARY * MONTHS DESC
LIMIT 1;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 1
entry.content:
Query a list of CITY and STATE from the STATION table.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

Solution

SELECT CITY, STATE FROM STATION;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 10
entry.content:
Problem

Query the list of CITY names from STATION that do not end with vowels. Your result cannot contain duplicates.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

This is a very basic problem using regular expression and ‘LIKE’.

Solution

SELECT DISTINCT CITY
FROM STATION
WHERE CITY LIKE '%[^AEIOU]';

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 11
entry.content:
Problem

Query the list of CITY names from STATION that either do not start with vowels or do not end with vowels. Your result cannot contain duplicates.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • This is a very basic problem using regular expression and ‘LIKE’.
  • Since result cannot contain duplicates, we also use ‘DISTINCT’.
  • Either do not start with vowels or do not end with vowels means it can not be simultaneously starting and ending with vowels.

Solution

SELECT DISTINCT CITY
FROM STATION
WHERE CITY NOT LIKE '[AEIOU]%[AEIOU]';

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 12
entry.content:
Problem

Query the list of CITY names from STATION that do not start with vowels and do not end with vowels. Your result cannot contain duplicates.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • This is a very basic problem using regular expression and ‘LIKE’.
  • Since result cannot contain duplicates, we also use ‘DISTINCT’.

Solution

SELECT DISTINCT CITY
FROM STATION
WHERE CITY LIKE '[^AEIOU]%[^AEIOU]';

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 13
entry.content:
Problem

Query the sum of Northern Latitudes (LAT_N) from STATION having values greater than 38.7880 and less than 137.2345. Truncate your answer to 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • Use SUM(LAT_N) to get the sum of Northern Latitudes.
  • To get values which are greater than 38.7880 and less than 137.2345, we use where clause WHERE LAT_N > 38.7880 AND LAT_N < 137.2345.
  • To truncate your answer to 4 decimal places, use TRUNCATE(number,4).

Solution

SELECT TRUNCATE(SUM(LAT_N),4) FROM STATION
WHERE LAT_N > 38.7880 AND LAT_N < 137.2345;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 14
entry.content:
Problem

Query the greatest value of Northern Latitudes (LAT_N) from STATION having values less than 137.2345. Truncate your answer to 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • Use MAX(LAT_N) to get the greatest value of Northern Latitudes.
  • To get values which are less than 137.2345, we use where clause LAT_N < 137.2345.
  • To truncate your answer to 4 decimal places, use TRUNCATE(number,4).

Solution

SELECT TRUNCATE(MAX(LAT_N),4) FROM STATION
WHERE LAT_N < 137.2345;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 16
entry.content:
Problem

Query the smallest Northern Latitude (LAT_N) from STATION that is greater than 38.7780. Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • Use MIN(LAT_N) to get the smallest value of Northern Latitudes.
  • To get values which are greater than 38.7780, we use where clause WHERE LAT_N > 38.7780.
  • To Round your answer to 4 decimal places, use ROUND(number,4).

Solution

SELECT ROUND(MIN(LAT_N),4) FROM STATION
WHERE LAT_N > 38.7780;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 17
entry.content:
Problem

Query the Western Longitude (LONG_W) where the smallest Northern Latitude (LAT_N) in STATION is greater than 38.7780. Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • Use ORDER BY LAT_N LIMIT 1 to get the smallest value of Northern Latitudes.
  • To get values which are greater than 38.7780, we use where clause WHERE LAT_N > 38.7780.
  • To Round your answer to 4 decimal places, use ROUND(number,4).

Solution

SELECT ROUND(LONG_W,4) FROM STATION
WHERE LAT_N > 38.7780
ORDER BY LAT_N
LIMIT 1;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 2
entry.content:
Query the following two values from the STATION table:

  1. The sum of all values in LAT_N rounded to a scale of 2 decimal places.
  2. The sum of all values in LONG_W rounded to a scale of 2 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Output Format

Your results must be in the form:

lat lon

where lat is the sum of all values in LAT_N and lon is the sum of all values in LONG_W. Both results must be rounded to a scale of 2 decimal places.

Solution

SELECT ROUND(SUM(LAT_N), 2), ROUND(SUM(LONG_W), 2)
FROM STATION;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 15
entry.content:
Problem

Query the Western Longitude (LONG_W) for the largest Northern Latitude (LAT_N) in STATION that is less than 137.2345. Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

  • Use ORDER BY LAT_N DESC LIMIT 1 to get the greatest value of Northern Latitudes.
  • To get values which are less than 137.2345, we use where clause WHERE LAT_N < 137.2345.
  • To round your answer to 4 decimal places, use ROUND(LONG_W,4).

Solution

SELECT ROUND(LONG_W,4) FROM STATION
WHERE LAT_N < 137.2345
ORDER BY LAT_N DESC
LIMIT 1;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 20
entry.content:
Problem

A median is defined as a number separating the higher half of a data set from the lower half. Query the median of the Northern Latitudes (LAT_N) from STATION and round your answer to 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Solution

It does not work on Oracle.

SET @rowIndex := -1;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(t.LAT_N), 4) FROM(
	SELECT @rowIndex := @rowIndex+1 AS rowIndex, 
		s.LAT_N FROM STATION AS s ORDER BY s.LAT_N
) AS t
WHERE t.rowIndex IN (FLOOR(@rowIndex / 2), CEIL(@rowIndex / 2));

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 18
entry.content:
Problem

Consider $P_1(a,b)$ and $P_2(c,d)$ to be two points on a 2D plane.

  • $a$ happens to equal the minimum value in Northern Latitude (LAT_N in STATION).
  • $b$ happens to equal the minimum value in Western Longitude (LONG_W in STATION).
  • $c$ happens to equal the maximum value in Northern Latitude (LAT_N in STATION).
  • $d$ happens to equal the maximum value in Western Longitude (LONG_W in STATION).

Query the Manhattan Distance between points $P_1$ and $P_2$ and round it to a scale of 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

The Manhattan Distance is given by $|a-c|+|b-d|$. Note in this case, we have $a \leqslant c$ and $b \leqslant d$. Hence it should be relatively easy.

  • $a$, $b$, $c$, $d$ are given by MIN(LAT_N), MIN(LONG_W), MAX(LAT_N), MAX(LONG_W). It is clear that Manhattan Distance is given by
    MAX(LAT_N)-MIN(LAT_N)+MAX(LONG_W)-MIN(LONG_W)
  • To Round your answer to 4 decimal places, use ROUND(number,4).

Solution

SELECT ROUND(MAX(LAT_N)-MIN(LAT_N)+MAX(LONG_W)-MIN(LONG_W),4)
FROM STATION;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 3
entry.content:
Query a list of CITY names from STATION with even ID numbers only. You may print the results in any order, but must exclude duplicates from your answer.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Solution

SELECT DISTINCT CITY FROM STATION
WHERE ID % 2 = 0;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 19
entry.content:
Problem

Consider $P_1(a,b)$ and $P_2(c,d)$ to be two points on a 2D plane.

  • $a$ happens to equal the minimum value in Northern Latitude (LAT_N in STATION).
  • $b$ happens to equal the minimum value in Western Longitude (LONG_W in STATION).
  • $c$ happens to equal the maximum value in Northern Latitude (LAT_N in STATION).
  • $d$ happens to equal the maximum value in Western Longitude (LONG_W in STATION).

Query the Euclidean Distance between points $P_1$ and $P_2$ and round it to a scale of 4 decimal places.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Idea

The Euclidean Distance is given by $\sqrt{(a-c)^2+(b-d)^2}$. The idea should be relatively easy.

  • $a$, $b$, $c$, $d$ are given by MIN(LAT_N), MIN(LONG_W), MAX(LAT_N), MAX(LONG_W). It is clear that Euclidean Distance is given by
    SQRT( 
    (MAX(LAT_N)-MIN(LAT_N))*(MAX(LAT_N)-MIN(LAT_N)) + 
    (MAX(LONG_W)-MIN(LONG_W))*(MAX(LONG_W)-MIN(LONG_W)) 
)
  • To Round your answer to 4 decimal places, use ROUND(number,4).

Solution

SELECT ROUND( 
    SQRT( 
        (MAX(LAT_N)-MIN(LAT_N))*(MAX(LAT_N)-MIN(LAT_N)) + 
        (MAX(LONG_W)-MIN(LONG_W))*(MAX(LONG_W)-MIN(LONG_W)) 
    ), 4)
FROM STATION;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 4
entry.content:
Let $N$ be the number of CITY entries in STATION, and let $N’$ be the number of distinct CITY names in STATION; query the value of $N-N’$ from STATION. In other words, find the difference between the total number of CITY entries in the table and the number of distinct CITY entries in the table.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Solution

SELECT COUNT(CITY) - COUNT(DISTINCT CITY)
FROM STATION;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Weather Observation Station 5
entry.content:
Query the two cities in STATION with the shortest and longest CITY names, as well as their respective lengths (i.e.: number of characters in the name). If there is more than one smallest or largest city, choose the one that comes first when ordered alphabetically.

Input Format

The STATION table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
CITY VARCHAR2(21)
STATE VARCHAR2(2)
LAT_N NUMBER
LONG_W NUMBER

where LAT_N is the northern latitude and LONG_W is the western longitude.

Sample Input

Let’s say that CITY only has four entries: DEF, ABC, PQRS and WXY

Sample Output

ABC 3
PQRS 4
Explanation

When ordered alphabetically, the CITY names are listed as ABC, DEF, PQRS, and WXY, with the respective lengths 3, 3, 4, and 3. The longest-named city is obviously PQRS, but there are options for shortest-named city; we choose ABC, because it comes first alphabetically.

Note
You can write two separate queries to get the desired output. It need not be a single query.

Solution

Becareful it should be LENGTH, not LEN!

SELECT CITY, LENGTH(CITY)
FROM STATION 
ORDER BY LENGTH(CITY), CITY
LIMIT 1;

SELECT CITY, LENGTH(CITY)
FROM STATION 
ORDER BY LENGTH(CITY) DESC, CITY ASC
LIMIT 1;

✧ Type of Triangle

复习 ‘condition’ 咋写!

SELECT
CASE
    WHEN (A+B-C)*(A-B+C)*(-A+B+C)<=0 THEN 'Not A Triangle'
    WHEN A=B AND A=C THEN 'Equilateral'
    WHEN (A-B)*(B-C)*(C-A)=0 THEN 'Isosceles'
    ELSE 'Scalene'
END
FROM TRIANGLES;

)

FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES
WHERE @TEMP < 20;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Higher Than 75 Marks
entry.content:
Problem

Query the Name of any student in STUDENTS who scored higher than 75 Marks. Order your output by the last three characters of each name. If two or more students both have names ending in the same last three characters (i.e.: Bobby, Robby, etc.), secondary sort them by ascending ID.

Input Format

The STUDENTS table is described as follows:

Column Type
ID Integer
Name String
Marks Integer

The Name column only contains uppercase (A-Z) and lowercase (a-z) letters.

Sample Input

ID NAME MARKS
1 Ashley 81
2 Samantha 75
4 Julia 76
3 Belvet 84

Sample Output

Ashley
Julia
Belvet

Explanation

Only Ashley, Julia, and Belvet have Marks > 75. If you look at the last three characters of each of their names, there are no duplicates and ‘ley’ < ‘lia’ < ‘vet’.

Idea

  • Use where clause WHERE MARKS > 75 to get students who scored higher than 75 Marks.
  • Sort by RIGHT(NAME, 3) and ID.

Solution

SELECT NAME FROM STUDENTS
WHERE MARKS > 75
ORDER BY RIGHT(NAME, 3) ASC, ID;

entry.title: LeetCode 22. Generate Parentheses 06/16/2021
entry.content:
06/16/2021

My solution.

class Solution:
    def generateParenthesis(self, n: int) -> List[str]:
        res = [["()"]] * n
        i = 0
        while i < n - 1:
            res[i + 1] = ["(" + x + ")" for x in res[i]]
            for j in range(i + 1):
                for x in res[j]:
                    for y in res[i - j]:
                        if x + y not in res[i + 1]:
                            res[i + 1] = res[i + 1] + [x + y]
            i += 1
        return res[-1]

A better solution

class Solution:
    def generateParenthesis(self, n: int) -> List[str]:
        dp = [[] for i in range(n + 1)]
        dp[0].append('')
        for i in range(n + 1):
            for j in range(i):
                dp[i] += ['(' + x + ')' + y for x in dp[j] for y in dp[i - j - 1]]
        return dp[n]

entry.title: LeetCode 473. Matchsticks to Square 06/15/2021
entry.content:
06/15/2021

class Solution:
    def makesquare(self, matchsticks: List[int]) -> bool:
        def dfs(nums, pos, target):
            if pos == len(nums): return True
            for i in range(4):
                if nums[pos] <= target[i]:
                    target[i] = target[i] - nums[pos]
                    if dfs(nums, pos + 1, target): return True
                    target[i] = target[i] + nums[pos]
            return False
        
        matchsticks.sort(reverse = True)
        if len(matchsticks) < 4 or sum(matchsticks) % 4 or matchsticks[0] > sum(matchsticks)// 4:
            return False
        target = [sum(matchsticks)// 4] * 4
        return dfs(matchsticks, 0, target)

entry.title: LeetCode 374. Guess Number Higher or Lower
entry.content:
06/14/2021

class Solution:
    def guessNumber(self, n: int) -> int:
        left, right = 1, n
        while left < right - 1:
            mid = left + (right + 1 -left) // 2
            if guess(mid) == 0: return mid
            if guess(mid) == -1: right = mid
            if guess(mid) == 1: left = mid
        if guess(left) == 0: return left
        return right

entry.title: Jump Game 篇
entry.content:
55. Jump Game

m 表示目前可以达到的最大的 index. 注意如果能够到 i, 必然能够到小于 i 的 index.

m = nums[0]
for i in range(1,len(nums)):
    if i > m:
        return False
    else:
        m = max(m, i + nums[i])
return True

从后面开始

goal = len(nums) - 1
for i in range(len(nums))[::-1]:
    if i + nums[i] >= goal:
        goal = i
return not goal

entry.title: LeetCode 455. Assign Cookies
entry.content:
06/14/2021

class Solution:
    def findContentChildren(self, g: List[int], s: List[int]) -> int:
        g_ind, s_ind, i =0, 0, 0
        g.sort()
        s.sort()
        while g_ind < len(g) and s_ind < len(s):
            if s[s_ind] >= g[g_ind]:
                i += 1
                s_ind += 1
                g_ind += 1
            elif s[s_ind] < g[g_ind]:
                s_ind += 1
        return i

Note that g_ind and i coincide. So we can safely replace g_ind with i.

class Solution:
    def findContentChildren(self, g: List[int], s: List[int]) -> int:
        s_ind, i =0, 0
        g.sort()
        s.sort()
        while i < len(g) and s_ind < len(s):
            if s[s_ind] >= g[i]:
                i += 1
            s_ind += 1
        return i

entry.title: LeetCode 492. Construct the Rectangle
entry.content:
06/14/2021

class Solution:
    def constructRectangle(self, area: int) -> List[int]:
        width = int(area ** 0.5)
        while width > 0:
            if not area % width: 
                return [area // width, width]
            width -= 1

entry.title: LeetCode 507. Perfect Number
entry.content:
06/14/2021

class Solution:    
    def checkPerfectNumber(self, num: int) -> bool:
        if num == 1: return False
        res, i = 1, 2
        while i <= num // i:
            if not num % i and i != num // i: 
                res += (i + num // i)
            elif not num % i: 
                res += i
            i += 1
        return res == num

entry.title: LeetCode 504. Base 7
entry.content:
06/14/2021

class Solution:
    def convertToBase7(self, num: int) -> str:
        n = abs(num)
        res = ''
        while n > 0:
            res = str(n % 7) + res
            n = n // 7
        if num == 0: return '0'
        if num > 0: return res
        if num < 0: return '-' + res

entry.title: LeetCode 1732. Find the Highest Altitude

06/15/2021

class Solution:
    def largestAltitude(self, gain: List[int]) -> int:
        res, height = 0, 0        
        for num in gain:
            height += num
            res = max(res, height)
        return res

One line!

class Solution:
    def largestAltitude(self, gain: List[int]) -> int:
        return max(0, max(accumulate(gain)))

entry.title: LeetCode 1897. Redistribute Characters to Make All Strings Equal
entry.content:
06/14/2021

It is true iff the frequency of every letter is a multiple of len(words).

class Solution:
    def makeEqual(self, words: List[str]) -> bool:
        freq = {}
        for word in words:
            for char in word:
                freq[char] = freq.get(char, 0) + 1
        
        for char in freq:
            if freq[char] % len(words):
                return False
        return True

It can be made one line with collections.Counter.

return all([ val % len(w) == 0 for val in collections.Counter(''.join(w)).values() ])

entry.title: LeetCode 1710. Maximum Units on a Truck 06/14/2021
entry.content:
06/14/2021

The idea is pretty much simple.

  • First, we sort boxTypes based on numberOfUnitsPerBox with reverse = True.
  • Then we start from the beginning, we modify truckSize by subtracting the minimum truckSize and the available numberOfBoxes.
  • We stop when truckSize == 0 or we run out all elements in boxTypes.
class Solution:
    def maximumUnits(self, boxTypes: List[List[int]], truckSize: int) -> int:
        boxTypes.sort(reverse = True, key = lambda x: x[1])
        units, i = 0, 0
        while i < len(boxTypes) and truckSize:
            adds = min(truckSize, boxTypes[i][0])
            units += adds * boxTypes[i][1]
            truckSize -= adds
            i += 1
        return units

entry.title: Leetcode - Array 篇
entry.content:
简单

53. Maximum Subarray

易错点, 如果所有的数都是负数, 可能得出最大值为零的错误.

class Solution:
    def maxSubArray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        
        maxsum, currsum = nums[0], 0
        
        if(max(nums)<0):
            return max(nums) #这一步关键
        
        for i in range(len(nums)):
            currsum += nums[i]
            if currsum < 0:
                currsum = 0
            maxsum = max(maxsum, currsum)
                    
        return maxsum

我给出的解答, 细微差别!

class Solution:
    def maxSubArray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        max_sub, curr_sub = nums[0], 0
        for num in nums:
            if curr_sub + num > 0:
                curr_sub += num
                max_sub = max(max_sub, curr_sub)
            else:
                max_sub = max(max_sub, num)
                curr_sub = 0
        
        return max_sub

88. Merge Sorted Array

此题关键是处理边界以及停止 while 的条件:

class Solution:
    def merge(self, nums1: List[int], m: int, nums2: List[int], n: int) -> None:
        """
        Do not return anything, modify nums1 in-place instead.
        """
        while m > 0 and n > 0:
            if nums1[m-1] > nums2[n-1]:
                nums1[m+n-1] = nums1[m-1]
                m -= 1
            else:
                nums1[m+n-1] = nums2[n-1]
                n -= 1
        
        if n > 0:
            nums1[:n] = nums2[:n]

如果使用 while m > 0 or n > 0 在边界时会出问题.

448. Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array

下面这种方法会超时

class Solution:
    def findDisappearedNumbers(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        return [x for x in range(1,len(nums)+1) if x not in nums]

另外两种方法

class Solution:
    def findDisappearedNumbers(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        
        return list(set(range(1, len(nums)+1))-set(nums))
class Solution(object):
    def findDisappearedNumbers(self, nums):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :rtype: List[int]
        """
        # For each number i in nums,
        # we mark the number that i points as negative.
        # Then we filter the list, get all the indexes
        # who points to a positive number
        for i in range(len(nums)):
            index = abs(nums[i]) - 1
            nums[index] = - abs(nums[index])

        return [i + 1 for i in range(len(nums)) if nums[i] > 0]

643. Maximum Average Subarray I

class Solution:
    def findMaxAverage(self, nums: List[int], k: int) -> float:
        sums = sum(nums[0:k])
        res = sums / k
        for i in range(1,len(nums) - k + 1):
            sums = sums - nums[i-1] + nums[i+k-1]     #这一步是关键, 如果每次都是直接求和会超时!           
            res = max(sums/ k, res)
        return res

704. Binary Search 典型二分法

class Solution:
    def search(self, nums: List[int], target: int) -> int:
        left, right = 0, len(nums) - 1
        while left <= right:
            mid = (left + right) // 2
            if nums[mid] == target:  # 此处分三步处理,而不是简单的 if-else!if-else!
                return mid
            elif nums[mid] < target:
                left = mid + 1
            else:
                right = mid - 1
        return -1

746 Min Cost Climbing Stairs

如果用 recursion 解决, 通常不是最佳答案, 需要重新考虑解法, 类似地还有斐波那契数列. 容易超时!

class Solution:
    def minCostClimbingStairs(self, cost: List[int]) -> int:
        if len(cost) < 3:
            return min(cost)
        elif len(cost) == 3:
            return min(cost[0]+cost[2], cost[1])
        else:
            return min(self.minCostClimbingStairs(cost[:len(cost)-1]) + cost[len(cost)-1], 
                self.minCostClimbingStairs(cost[:len(cost)-2]) + cost[len(cost)-2])

DP - Dynamic Programming

class Solution:
    def minCostClimbingStairs(self, cost: List[int]) -> int:
        dp1, dp2 = 0, 0
        for i in range(len(cost)):
            dp1, dp2 = dp2, cost[i] + min(dp1, dp2)
            
        return min(dp1, dp2)

922. Sort Array By Parity II

class Solution:
    def sortArrayByParityII(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        odd, even = [], []
        for num in nums:
            if num % 2: odd.append(num)
            else: even.append(num)
        res =[0 for _ in nums]
        for i in range(len(odd)):
            res[2*i] = even[i]
            res[2*i+1] = odd[i]
        
        return res

双指针 in-place

class Solution:
    def sortArrayByParityII(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        i, j = 0, 1
        while i < len(nums) and j < len(nums):
            if not (nums[i] - i) % 2: i += 2
            elif not (nums[j] - j) % 2: j += 2
            else: 
                nums[i], nums[j] = nums[j], nums[i]
                i += 2
                j += 2
        return nums

941. Valid Mountain Array

我的答案超时了, 555

class Solution:
    def validMountainArray(self, arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        if len(arr) < 3:
            return False
        elif len(arr) == 3:
            return (arr[0] < arr[1]) and (arr[1] > arr[2])
        elif (arr[0] < arr[1]) and (arr[1] < arr[2]):
            return self.validMountainArray(arr[1:])
        elif (arr[-1] < arr[-2]) and (arr[-3] > arr[-2]):
            return self.validMountainArray(arr[:-1])
        else:
            return False

第一个通过的码

class Solution:
    def validMountainArray(self, arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        if len(arr) < 3:
            return False
        else:
            left, right = 0, len(arr) -1
            while left < right and arr[left] < arr[left + 1]:
                left += 1
            while left < right and arr[right] < arr[right - 1]:
                right -= 1
            return (left == right) and (left != 0) and (right != len(arr) -1)

适当精简

class Solution:
    def validMountainArray(self, arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        i, j, n = 0, len(arr) - 1, len(arr)
        while i + 1 < n and arr[i] < arr[i + 1]: i += 1
        while j > 0 and arr[j - 1] > arr[j]: j -= 1
        return 0 < i == j < n - 1

1475. Final Prices With a Special Discount in a Shop

这里用 stack 用得应该不对!

class Solution:
    def finalPrices(self, prices: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        if len(prices) == 1: return prices
        i = len(prices) - 1
        res = prices[:]
        stack = []
        while i >= 0:
            for num in stack[::-1]:
                if num <= prices[i]: 
                    res[i] = prices[i] - num
                    break #这个不要漏掉!
            while stack and stack[-1] >= prices[i]:
                stack.pop()
            stack.append(prices[i])
            i -= 1
        return res

懵逼脸! 直接用两个 for loop 也没慢多少…

class Solution:
    def finalPrices(self, prices: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        lp=len(prices)
        ret=[]
        for i in range(lp):
            for j in range(i+1,lp):
                if prices[j]<=prices[i]:
                    ret+=[prices[i]-prices[j]]
                    break
                if j==lp-1:
                    ret+=[prices[i]]
        ret.append(prices[-1])
        return ret
class Solution:
    def finalPrices(self, prices: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        stack = []
        for i, a in enumerate(prices):
            while stack and prices[stack[-1]] >= a:
                prices[stack.pop()] -= a
            stack.append(i)
        return prices

中等

困难

entry.title: LeetCode 1323. Maximum 69 Number
entry.content:
06/15/2021

class Solution:
    def maximum69Number (self, num: int) -> int:
        i, n, ind = 0, num, -1
        while n > 0:
            if n % 10 == 6:
                ind = i
            i += 1
            n = n // 10
        return num + 3 * 10 ** ind if ind > -1 else num

If we transform num to string.

class Solution:
    def maximum69Number (self, num: int) -> int:
        s = list(str(num))
        for i, char in enumerate(s):
            if char == '6':
                s[i] = '9'
                break
        return int(''.join(s))

One line solution!

class Solution:
    def maximum69Number (self, num: int) -> int:
        return int(str(num).replace('6', '9', 1))

entry.title: Leetcode 动态规划篇
entry.content:
简单

70. 爬楼梯

class Solution:
    def climbStairs(self, n: int) -> int:
        d = [1] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(2, n + 1):
            d[i] = d[i-1] +d[i-2]
        return d[n]

或者

class Solution:
    def climbStairs(self, n: int) -> int:
        a, b = 1, 1
        for i in range(1,n):
            a, b = b, a + b            
        return b

509. 斐波那契数

不要用迭代, 会超时!

class Solution:
    def fib(self, n: int) -> int:
        a, b = 0, 1
        i = 0
        while i < n:
            a, b = b, a + b
            i += 1
        return a

★ 746. 使用最小花费爬楼梯

不要用迭代, 会超时!

class Solution:
    def minCostClimbingStairs(self, cost: List[int]) -> int:
        if len(cost) == 2: 
            return min(cost[0], cost[1])
        elif len(cost) == 3:
            return min(cost[0]+cost[2], cost[1])
        else: 
            return min(cost[-1]+self.minCostClimbingStairs(cost[0:len(cost)-1]),
                  cost[-2]+self.minCostClimbingStairs(cost[0:len(cost)-2]))

考虑清楚再写! 当然也可以不用数组, 就用两个变量.

class Solution:
    def minCostClimbingStairs(self, cost: List[int]) -> int:
        d = [0] * (len(cost) + 1)
        i = 2
        for i in range(2,len(d)):
            d[i] = min(d[i-1]+cost[i-1], d[i-2]+cost[i-2])
        return d[-1]

稍微不同点的版本 

class Solution:
    def minCostClimbingStairs(self, cost: List[int]) -> int:
        dp = [0] * (len(cost))
        dp[0] = cost[0]
        dp[1] = cost[1]
        for i in range(2, len(cost)):
            dp[i] = min(dp[i - 1], dp[i - 2]) + cost[i]
        return min(dp[len(cost) - 1], dp[len(cost) - 2])

中等

62.不同路径

简单的组合题.

class Solution:
    def uniquePaths(self, m: int, n: int) -> int:
        dp = [[1] * n] * m
        for i in range(1, m):
            for j in range(1, n):
                dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]
        return dp[-1][-1]

63.不同路径II

下面这个有问题, 而且问题出在一个非常奇怪的地方.

class Solution:
    def uniquePathsWithObstacles(self, obstacleGrid: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        m, n = len(obstacleGrid), len(obstacleGrid[0])
        dp = [[0] * n] * m  #这个不行!
        dp[0][0] = 1 if obstacleGrid[0][0] != 1 else 0
        if dp[0][0] == 0: 
            return 0
        
        for i in range(1, m):
            if obstacleGrid[i][0] != 1: 
                dp[i][0] = dp[i-1][0]
                
        for j in range(1, n):
            if obstacleGrid[0][j] != 1: 
                dp[0][j] = dp[0][j-1]     
                
        for i in range(1, m):
            for j in range(1, n):
                if obstacleGrid[i][j] != 1:
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]
        return dp[-1][-1]

适当修改就可以了.

class Solution:
    def uniquePathsWithObstacles(self, obstacleGrid: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        m, n = len(obstacleGrid), len(obstacleGrid[0])
        dp = [[0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
        dp[0][0] = 1 if obstacleGrid[0][0] != 1 else 0
        if dp[0][0] == 0: 
            return 0
        
        for i in range(1, m):
            if obstacleGrid[i][0] != 1: 
                dp[i][0] = dp[i-1][0]
                
        for j in range(1, n):
            if obstacleGrid[0][j] != 1: 
                dp[0][j] = dp[0][j-1]     
                
        for i in range(1, m):
            for j in range(1, n):
                if obstacleGrid[i][j] != 1:
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]
        return dp[-1][-1]

795. 区间子数组个数

这里的思路很明显, 需要考虑以 A[i] 为结尾的数组个数(如果从前到后遍历的话). 下面第三种情形是关键!

Suppose dp[i] denotes the number of valid sub-array ending with A[i]. We use following example to illustrate the idea:

A = [2, 1, 4, 2, 3], L = 2, R = 3

  1. if A[i] < L: For example, i = 1. We can only append A[i] to a valid sub-array ending with A[i-1] to create new sub-array. So we have dp[i] = dp[i-1] (for i > 0)

  2. if A[i] > R: For example, i = 2. No valid sub-array ending with A[i] exist. So we have dp[i] = 0. We also record the position of the invalid number 4 here as prev.

  3. if L <= A[i] <= R: For example, i = 4. In this case any sub-array starts after the previous invalid number to A[i] is a new valid sub-array. So dp[i] += i - prev. 关键步骤!

class Solution:
    def numSubarrayBoundedMax(self, nums: List[int], left: int, right: int) -> int:
        res, dp, prev = 0, 0, -1
        for i, num in enumerate(nums):
            if num > right:
                dp, prev = 0, i
            elif num >= left:
                dp = i - prev
            res += dp
        return res

困难

entry.title: Leetcode - Binary Search 篇
entry.content:
简单

852. Peak Index in a Mountain Array

class Solution:
    def peakIndexInMountainArray(self, arr: List[int]) -> int:
        left, right = 0, len(arr) - 1
        while left < right:
            mid = left + (right - left)//2
            if arr[mid] < arr[mid + 1]:
                left = mid + 1
            else:
                right = mid
        return left

中等

困难

entry.title: LeetCode SQL 篇
entry.content:
简单

175. Combine Two Tables

SELECT P.FIRSTNAME, P.LASTNAME, A.CITY, A.STATE 
FROM PERSON AS P
LEFT JOIN ADDRESS AS A ON P.PERSONID = A.PERSONID;

176. Second Highest Salary

方法一

SELECT 
    IFNULL((SELECT DISTINCT SALARY FROM EMPLOYEE
     ORDER BY SALARY DESC
     LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1) , NULL)
     AS SecondHighestSalary;

方法二

SELECT MAX(SALARY) AS SecondHighestSalary FROM EMPLOYEE
WHERE SALARY < (SELECT MAX(SALARY) FROM EMPLOYEE);

扩展: 如果求第三高呢?

SELECT 
(   
    SELECT MAX(Salary) 
    FROM EMPLOYEE 
    WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM Employee WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM Employee))
)
AS "ThirdHighestSalary"

181. Employees Earning More Than Their Managers

SELECT E1.NAME AS Employee FROM EMPLOYEE E1
JOIN EMPLOYEE E2 ON E1.MANAGERID = E2.ID
WHERE E1.SALARY > E2.SALARY;

似乎不用 Join 比较快??? 可能我是错的.

Select E1.Name as Employee
from Employee as E1, Employee as E2 
where E1.ManagerId = E2.Id and E1.Salary > E2.Salary;

182. Duplicate Emails

SELECT Email FROM Person 
GROUP BY Email
Having count(*) > 1;

183. Customers Who Never Order

SELECT C.NAME AS CUSTOMERS 
FROM Customers C
LEFT JOIN ORDERS O ON C.ID=O.CustomerId
WHERE O.ID IS NULL;

✧ 196. Delete Duplicate Emails

DELETE p1 FROM Person p1, Person p2
WHERE p1.Email = p2.Email AND p1.Id > p2.Id;

✧ 197. Rising Temperature

SELECT W2.ID FROM WEATHER W1, WEATHER W2
WHERE TO_DAYS(W1.RECORDDATE) + 1 = TO_DAYS(W2.RECORDDATE) AND W1.TEMPERATURE < W2.TEMPERATURE;

595. Big Countries

SELECT NAME, POPULATION, AREA FROM WORLD
WHERE POPULATION > 25000000 or AREA > 3000000;

596. Classes More Than 5 Students

SELECT CLASS FROM COURSES
GROUP BY CLASS
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT STUDENT) >= 5;

620. Not Boring Movies

SELECT ID, MOVIE, DESCRIPTION, RATING FROM CINEMA
WHERE DESCRIPTION != 'BORING' AND ID % 2 > 0
ORDER BY RATING DESC;

627. Swap Salary

UPDATE SALARY
SET SEX = (
    CASE
        WHEN SEX = 'f' THEN  'm'
        ELSE 'f'
    END
)

✧ 1179. Reformat Department Table

select id, 
    max(case when month = 'jan' then revenue else null end) as Jan_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'feb' then revenue else null end) as Feb_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'mar' then revenue else null end) as Mar_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'apr' then revenue else null end) as Apr_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'may' then revenue else null end) as May_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'jun' then revenue else null end) as Jun_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'jul' then revenue else null end) as Jul_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'aug' then revenue else null end) as Aug_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'sep' then revenue else null end) as Sep_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'oct' then revenue else null end) as Oct_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'nov' then revenue else null end) as Nov_Revenue,
    max(case when month = 'dec' then revenue else null end) as Dec_Revenue
from department
group by id
order by id

中等

177. Nth Highest Salary

LIMIT 1 OFFSET M 可以用 LIMIT M, 1 替代!

CREATE FUNCTION getNthHighestSalary(N INT) RETURNS INT
BEGIN
DECLARE M INT;
SET M=N-1;
  RETURN (
      # Write your MySQL query statement below.
        SELECT DISTINCT SALARY FROM EMPLOYEE ORDER BY SALARY DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET M
  );
END

困难

entry.title: Leetcode 刷题资源
entry.content:
Data Structure

  • Introduction to Data Structure by Leetcode

entry.title: Python 学习笔记
entry.content:
如果出现多个 return, 以第一个出现的 return 为准!

float('inf') 代表无穷大, 可以认为是 infinity.

range() 的 ‘step’ 不能是 -1.

In Python, slices are handled safely with respect to indices that are out of bounds. 但是单个 [k] 这样的就不行!

Logical Operators

To evaluate the expression <left> and <right>:

  1. Evaluate the subexpression <left>.
  2. If the result is a false value v, then the expression evaluates to v.
  3. Otherwise, the expression evaluates to the value of the subexpression <right>.

To evaluate the expression <left> or <right>:

  1. Evaluate the subexpression <left>.
  2. If the result is a true value v, then the expression evaluates to v.
  3. Otherwise, the expression evaluates to the value of the subexpression <right>.

BitwiseOperators

  • x << y Returns x with the bits shifted to the left by y places (and new bits on the right-hand-side are zeros). This is the same as multiplying x by 2**y.

  • x >> y Returns x with the bits shifted to the right by y places. This is the same as //‘ing x by 2**y.

  • x & y Does a “bitwise and”. Each bit of the output is 1 if the corresponding bit of x AND of y is 1, otherwise it’s 0.

  • x | y Does a “bitwise or”. Each bit of the output is 0 if the corresponding bit of x AND of y is 0, otherwise it’s 1.

  • ~ x Returns the complement of x - the number you get by switching each 1 for a 0 and each 0 for a 1. This is the same as -x - 1.

  • x ^ y Does a “bitwise exclusive or”. Each bit of the output is the same as the corresponding bit in x if that bit in y is 0, and it’s the complement of the bit in x if that bit in y is 1.

zip( ) function

>>> a = [1,2,3]
>>> b = [4,5,6]
>>> c = [4,5,6,7,8]
>>> zipped = zip(a,b)     # 打包为元组的列表
[(1, 4), (2, 5), (3, 6)]
>>> zip(a,c)              # 元素个数与最短的列表一致
[(1, 4), (2, 5), (3, 6)]
>>> zip(*zipped)          # 与 zip 相反，*zipped 可理解为解压，返回二维矩阵式
[(1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6)]

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Revising the Select Query II
entry.content:
Query the names of all American cities in CITY with populations larger than 120000. The CountryCode for America is USA.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

Solution

SELECT NAME
FROM CITY
WHERE COUNTRYCODE = 'USA' AND POPULATION > 120000;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Revising the Select Query I

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

FROM CITY
WHERE COUNTRYCODE = 'USA' AND POPULATION > 100000;

entry.title: SQL Hackerrank Select All
entry.content:
Query all columns (attributes) for every row in the CITY table.

Input Format

The CITY table is described as follows:

Field Type
ID NUMBER
NAME VARCHAR2(17)
COUNTRYCODE VARCHAR2(3)
DISTRICT VARCHAR2(20)
POPULATION NUMBER

Solution

SELECT * FROM CITY;
